name: Vector3d
summary: Represents the three components of a vector in three-dimensional space, using Double-precision floating point numbers.

constructors.name: Vector3d(Point3d)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of a vector, copying the three components from the three coordinates of a point.

constructors.name: Vector3d(Vector3d)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of a vector, copying the three components from a vector.

constructors.name: Vector3d(Vector3f)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of a vector, copying the three components from a single-precision vector.

constructors.name: Vector3d(Double, Double, Double)
constructors.signature: public Vector3d(double x, double y, double z)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of a vector, using its three components.

methods.name: Add
methods.signature: public static Vector3d Add(Vector3d vector1, Vector3d vector2)
methods.summary: Sums up two vectors. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the + operator otherwise)

returns.doc: A new vector that results from the component-wise addition of the two vectors.

methods.name: AreOrthogonal
methods.signature: public static bool AreOrthogonal(Vector3d x, Vector3d y, Vector3d z)
methods.summary: Test whether three vectors describe an orthogonal axis system. All vectors must be mutually perpendicular this to be the case.

returns.doc: True if all vectors are non-zero and mutually perpendicular.

methods.name: AreOrthonormal
methods.signature: public static bool AreOrthonormal(Vector3d x, Vector3d y, Vector3d z)
methods.summary: Test whether three vectors describe an orthogonal, unit axis system. All vectors must be mutually perpendicular and have unit length for this to be the case.

returns.doc: True if all vectors are non-zero and mutually perpendicular.

methods.name: AreRighthanded
methods.signature: public static bool AreRighthanded(Vector3d x, Vector3d y, Vector3d z)
methods.summary: Test whether three vectors describe a right-handed, orthogonal, unit axis system. The vectors must be orthonormal and follow the right-hand ordering; index-finger=x, middle-finger=y, thumb=z.

returns.doc: True if all vectors are non-zero and mutually perpendicular.

methods.name: CompareTo
methods.signature: public int CompareTo(Vector3d other)
methods.summary: Compares this Vector3d with another Vector3d. Component evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

params.name: other
params.doc: The other Vector3d to use in comparison.

returns.doc: 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this.X < other.X-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y < other.Y-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y == other.Y and this.Z < other.Z+1: otherwise.

methods.name: CrossProduct
methods.signature: public static Vector3d CrossProduct(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)
methods.summary: Computes the cross product (or vector product, or exterior product) of two vectors. This operation is not commutative.

returns.doc: A new vector that is perpendicular to both a and b, has Length == a.Length * b.Length * sin(theta) where theta is the angle between a and b.The resulting vector is oriented according to the right hand rule.

methods.name: Divide
methods.signature: public static Vector3d Divide(Vector3d vector, double t)
methods.summary: Divides a Vector3d by a number, having the effect of shrinking it. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the / operator otherwise)

returns.doc: A new vector that is component-wise divided by t.

methods.name: EpsilonEquals
methods.signature: public bool EpsilonEquals(Vector3d other, double epsilon)
methods.summary: Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d,System.Double)"]

methods.name: Equals(Object)
methods.signature: public override bool Equals(Object obj)
methods.summary: Determines whether the specified System.Object is a Vector3d and has the same values as the present vector.

returns.doc: true if obj is a Vector3d and has the same coordinates as this; otherwise false.

methods.name: Equals(Vector3d)
methods.signature: public bool Equals(Vector3d vector)
methods.summary: Determines whether the specified vector has the same value as the present vector.

returns.doc: true if vector has the same coordinates as this; otherwise false.

methods.name: GetHashCode
methods.signature: public override int GetHashCode()
methods.summary: Computes the hash code for the current vector.

returns.doc: A non-unique number that represents the components of this vector.

methods.name: IsParallelTo(Vector3d)
methods.signature: public int IsParallelTo(Vector3d other)
methods.summary: Determines whether this vector is parallel to another vector, within one degree (within Pi / 180).

returns.doc: Parallel indicator: +1 = both vectors are parallel 0 = vectors are not parallel, or at least one of the vectors is zero-1 = vectors are anti-parallel.

methods.name: IsParallelTo(Vector3d, Double)
methods.signature: public int IsParallelTo(Vector3d other, double angleTolerance)
methods.summary: Determines whether this vector is parallel to another vector, within a provided tolerance.

returns.doc: Parallel indicator: +1 = both vectors are parallel.0 = vectors are not parallel or at least one of the vectors is zero.-1 = vectors are anti-parallel.

methods.name: IsPerpendicularTo(Vector3d)
methods.signature: public bool IsPerpendicularTo(Vector3d other)
methods.summary: Test to see whether this vector is perpendicular to within one degree of another one.

returns.doc: true if both vectors are perpendicular, false if otherwise.

methods.name: IsPerpendicularTo(Vector3d, Double)
methods.signature: public bool IsPerpendicularTo(Vector3d other, double angleTolerance)
methods.summary: Determines whether this vector is perpendicular to another vector, within a provided angle tolerance.

returns.doc: true if vectors form Pi-radians (90-degree) angles with each other; otherwise false.

methods.name: IsTiny()
methods.signature: public bool IsTiny()
methods.summary: Uses RhinoMath.ZeroTolerance for IsTiny calculation.

returns.doc: true if vector is very small, otherwise false.

methods.name: IsTiny(Double)
methods.signature: public bool IsTiny(double tolerance)
methods.summary: Determines whether a vector is very short.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: A nonzero value used as the coordinate zero tolerance. .

returns.doc: (Math.Abs(X) <= tiny_tol) AND (Math.Abs(Y) <= tiny_tol) AND (Math.Abs(Z) <= tiny_tol).

methods.name: Multiply(Double, Vector3d)
methods.signature: public static Vector3d Multiply(double t, Vector3d vector)
methods.summary: Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

returns.doc: A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

methods.name: Multiply(Vector3d, Vector3d)
methods.signature: public static double Multiply(Vector3d vector1, Vector3d vector2)
methods.summary: Multiplies two vectors together, returning the dot product (or inner product). This differs from the cross product. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

returns.doc: A value that results from the evaluation of v1.X*v2.X + v1.Y*v2.Y + v1.Z*v2.Z. This value equals v1.Length * v2.Length * cos(alpha), where alpha is the angle between vectors.

methods.name: Multiply(Vector3d, Double)
methods.signature: public static Vector3d Multiply(Vector3d vector, double t)
methods.summary: Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

returns.doc: A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

methods.name: Negate
methods.signature: public static Vector3d Negate(Vector3d vector)
methods.summary: Computes the reversed vector. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the - unary operator otherwise)
methods.remarks: Similar to Reverse(), but static for CLR compliance.

returns.doc: A new vector where all components were multiplied by -1.

methods.name: PerpendicularTo
methods.signature: public bool PerpendicularTo(Vector3d other)
methods.summary: Sets this vector to be perpendicular to another vector. Result is not unitized.

returns.doc: true on success, false if input vector is zero or invalid.

methods.name: Reverse
methods.signature: public bool Reverse()
methods.summary: Reverses this vector in place (reverses the direction). If this vector is Invalid, no changes will occur and false will be returned.
methods.remarks: Similar to Negate, that is only provided for CLR language compliance.

returns.doc: true on success or false if the vector is invalid.

methods.name: Rotate
methods.signature: public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis)
methods.summary: Rotates this vector around a given axis.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: Subtract
methods.signature: public static Vector3d Subtract(Vector3d vector1, Vector3d vector2)
methods.summary: Subtracts the second vector from the first one. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the - operator otherwise)

returns.doc: A new vector that results from the component-wise difference of vector1 - vector2.

methods.name: ToString()
methods.signature: public override string ToString()
methods.summary: Returns the string representation of the current vector, in the form X,Y,Z.

returns.doc: A string with the current location of the point.

methods.name: ToString(String, IFormatProvider)
methods.signature: public string ToString(string format, IFormatProvider formatProvider)
methods.summary: Formats the value of the current instance using the specified format.

params.name: format
params.doc: The format to use. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to use the default format defined for the type of the IFormattable implementation.

params.name: formatProvider
params.doc: The provider to use to format the value. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to obtain the numeric format information from the current locale setting of the operating system.

returns.doc: The value of the current instance in the specified format.

methods.name: Transform
methods.signature: public void Transform(Transform transformation)
methods.summary: Transforms the vector in place. The transformation matrix acts on the left of the vector; i.e.,result = transformation*vector

methods.name: Unitize
methods.signature: public bool Unitize()
methods.summary: Unitizes the vector in place. A unit vector has length 1 unit. An invalid or zero length vector cannot be unitized.

returns.doc: true on success or false on failure.

methods.name: VectorAngle(Vector3d, Vector3d)
methods.signature: public static double VectorAngle(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)
methods.summary: Compute the angle between two vectors. This operation is commutative.

returns.doc: If the input is valid, the angle (in radians) between a and b; RhinoMath.UnsetValue otherwise.

methods.name: VectorAngle(Vector3d, Vector3d, Plane)
methods.signature: public static double VectorAngle(Vector3d a, Vector3d b, Plane plane)
methods.summary: Computes the angle on a plane between two vectors.

params.name: plane
params.doc: Two-dimensional plane on which to perform the angle measurement.

returns.doc: On success, the angle (in radians) between a and b as projected onto the plane; RhinoMath.UnsetValue on failure.

methods.name: VectorAngle(Vector3d, Vector3d, Vector3d)
methods.signature: public static double VectorAngle(Vector3d v1, Vector3d v2, Vector3d vNormal)
methods.summary: Computes the angle of v1, v2 with a normal vector.

returns.doc: On success, the angle (in radians) between a and b with respect of normal vector; RhinoMath.UnsetValue on failure.

methods.name: Addition
methods.signature: public static Vector3d operator +(Vector3d vector1, Vector3d vector2)
methods.summary: Sums up two vectors.

returns.doc: A new vector that results from the component-wise addition of the two vectors.

methods.name: Division
methods.signature: public static Vector3d operator /(Vector3d vector, double t)
methods.summary: Divides a Vector3d by a number, having the effect of shrinking it.

returns.doc: A new vector that is component-wise divided by t.

methods.name: Equality
methods.signature: public static bool operator ==(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)
methods.summary: Determines whether two vectors have the same value.

returns.doc: true if all coordinates are pairwise equal; false otherwise.

methods.name: GreaterThan
methods.signature: public static bool operator >(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the first specified vector comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second vector. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

returns.doc: true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is larger than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z is larger than b.Z; otherwise, false.

methods.name: GreaterThanOrEqual
methods.signature: public static bool operator >=(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the first specified vector comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second vector, or it is equal to it. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

returns.doc: true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is larger than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z >= b.Z; otherwise, false.

methods.name: Implicit(Vector3f to Vector3d)
methods.signature: public static implicit operator Vector3d (Vector3f vector)
methods.summary: Converts a single-precision (float) vector in a double-precision vector, without needing casting.

returns.doc: The same vector, expressed using double-precision values.

methods.name: Inequality
methods.signature: public static bool operator !=(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)
methods.summary: Determines whether two vectors have different values.

returns.doc: true if any coordinate pair is different; false otherwise.

methods.name: LessThan
methods.signature: public static bool operator <(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the first specified vector comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second vector. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

returns.doc: true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z is smaller than b.Z; otherwise, false.

methods.name: LessThanOrEqual
methods.signature: public static bool operator <=(Vector3d a, Vector3d b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the first specified vector comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second vector, or it is equal to it. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y, then Z.

returns.doc: true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y, or a.X == b.X and a.Y == b.Y and a.Z <= b.Z; otherwise, false.

methods.name: Multiply(Double, Vector3d)
methods.signature: public static Vector3d operator *(double t, Vector3d vector)
methods.summary: Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it.

returns.doc: A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

methods.name: Multiply(Vector3d, Vector3d)
methods.signature: public static double operator *(Vector3d vector1, Vector3d vector2)
methods.summary: Multiplies two vectors together, returning the dot product (or inner product). This differs from the cross product.

returns.doc: A value that results from the evaluation of v1.X*v2.X + v1.Y*v2.Y + v1.Z*v2.Z. This value equals v1.Length * v2.Length * cos(alpha), where alpha is the angle between vectors.

methods.name: Multiply(Vector3d, Double)
methods.signature: public static Vector3d operator *(Vector3d vector, double t)
methods.summary: Multiplies a vector by a number, having the effect of scaling it.

returns.doc: A new vector that is the original vector coordinate-wise multiplied by t.

methods.name: Subtraction
methods.signature: public static Vector3d operator -(Vector3d vector1, Vector3d vector2)
methods.summary: Subtracts the second vector from the first one.

returns.doc: A new vector that results from the component-wise difference of vector1 - vector2.

methods.name: UnaryNegation
methods.signature: public static Vector3d operator -(Vector3d vector)
methods.summary: Computes the opposite vector.

returns.doc: A new vector where all components were multiplied by -1.

properties.name: IsUnitVector
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this is a unit vector. A unit vector has length 1.

properties.name: IsValid
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this vector is valid. A valid vector must be formed of valid component values for x, y and z.

properties.name: IsZero
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the X, Y, and Z values are all equal to 0.0.

properties.name: Item
properties.summary: Gets or sets a vector component at the given index.

properties.name: Length
properties.summary: Computes the length (or magnitude, or size) of this vector. This is an application of Pythagoras' theorem. If this vector is invalid, its length is considered 0.

properties.name: MaximumCoordinate
properties.summary: Gets the largest (both positive and negative) component value in this vector.

properties.name: MinimumCoordinate
properties.summary: Gets the smallest (both positive and negative) component value in this vector.

properties.name: SquareLength
properties.summary: Computes the squared length (or magnitude, or size) of this vector. This is an application of Pythagoras' theorem. While the Length property checks for input validity, this property does not. You should check validity in advance, if this vector can be invalid.

properties.name: Unset
properties.summary: Gets the value of the vector with each component set to RhinoMath.UnsetValue.

properties.name: X
properties.summary: Gets or sets the X (first) component of the vector.

properties.name: XAxis
properties.summary: Gets the value of the vector with components 1,0,0.

properties.name: Y
properties.summary: Gets or sets the Y (second) component of the vector.

properties.name: YAxis
properties.summary: Gets the value of the vector with components 0,1,0.

properties.name: Z
properties.summary: Gets or sets the Z (third) component of the vector.

properties.name: ZAxis
properties.summary: Gets the value of the vector with components 0,0,1.

properties.name: Zero
properties.summary: Gets the value of the vector with components 0,0,0.
